item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't get this one AT ALL the first time I read it because I didn't bother to check the Discussion page. With your explanation it makes so much more sense (obviously), and it's such a relief to finally get it that I had to +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really is cool all the ways you can screw with wikidot formatting

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I found the "fake" version of the interview rather amusing. Reminds me of that type of cartoon character who just mumbles or beeps in place of enunciating actual speech, but is somehow understood perfectly by all the other characters anyway.

"Hello, welcome to Burger King; May I take your order?"

"You must be eliminated at all costs."

"A double whopper, a medium coke, and a side of fries. Anything else?"

"You must be eliminated at all costs."

"Oh, sorry. Large coke. Will that be all?"

"You must be eliminated at all costs."

"Thank you, please drive up to the first window."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Damn, the Chaos insurgency don't fuck around.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was a short but fun read. Upvoted.
If I could make any suggestions, I wonder if the SCP would attempt to un-redact A███ R███████'s name to make him an easier target to dox.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was confused at first and didnt understand the big deal of this article… Until I clicked the text and the document changed. Not sure how you did it, but its awesome.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
definite +1 from me.

Only possible way to improve this would be rewording the closing statement of the interview slightly. Termination is often insinuated to be death, while the closing statement seemed more like the termination of Davies' job. perhaps instead, "Dr. Davies' employment at the Foundation should end with his immediate termination.

Either way, great scp

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I agree. I liked the article without noticing the format screw, then the comments pointed it out so I went back but was disappointed by it. It feels gimmicky and, in my opinion, doesn't really add much to the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i was gonna downvote

then i read the comments

then i re-read

hoo boy updoot time

item.title: Random Clicking
item.content:encoded: I wasn't a big fan of this article until I accidentally clicked the white spaces. If on mobile click on the white spaces and on every bit of text to reveal the story. Format screws work in this instance for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow, I already enjoyed this article enough to upvote it before someone pointed out the formatting gimmick - this is a real gem.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i’m lucky to be a mobile user who impatiently clicks/highlights everything. well-deserved +1 for doing something so succinct and interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The site is absolutely on the other side of the hill when it comes to giant, heavy narratives, and there’s plenty of shorter articles that have come out recently that still manage to contextualize themselves better.

Also, the whole baby shoes bit might be an extremely short story, but it’s not a particularly good one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I gotta disagree. I miss the days when you could click on a scip and get a nice, quick little story. Everything seems to be so involved these days. 'Baby shoes for sale; never worn' type ideaslike these are getting rarer and rarer. This'll be a lot more memorable than a lot of its contemporaries because it's easily digestible and well written.

Just the right length, doesn't over stay it's welcome.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly, I think this would've been stronger without the format screw. Mostly because the base interview does enough (at least for me) on its own that I didn't even need the mouse-over text to figure out what was going on, and like… having the mouse over text to confirm it felt less satisfying than letting it play out in my head. I was more than amused enough without the format screw to +1, but seeing the format screw afterwards and seeing how it just sort of confirmed my suspicions instead of put a new spin or twist on the article makes this a much much weaker +1. Like, I would probably no-vote if I didn't really like the reading of this without the format screw.

Edit: actually, upon further thought, I am more disappointed by the lack of new spin/twist the format screw put on this than I originally thought I was so I'm gonna go with a novote.

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Noting that the image is indeed compliant with BY-SA 3.0.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gotta agree with pedantique. Could be served by having more information and a gradual buildup, it kinda just smacks you in the face with it. Perhaps a little more of the entity trying to convince the reader as to why it is dangerous or creating logs, or altering a posted interaction with the MTF mentioned in the article. But I like it, so I'll upvote even though I want a bit more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 for interesting use of formatting, and the story stands well enough on it's own. The "testing" element, and closing addendum feel a bit weak, however. You don't ever launch too far off of the core premise of this man being "marked", leaving the narrative a bit too open ended.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a neat gimmick, but I don't think the article itself is developed enough to stand without it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was mindlessly perusing not really paying attention.
Then your format voodoo magic caught my breath and attention.
Give them back, please I need them.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Some really great world building for the CI, which in my opinion has fallen by the wayside outside of the recent 001 proposal. Great work with that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Homebrew version of this article’s formatting time!

This article must be eliminated at all costs.1

Footnotes
1. Though the idea’s mediocre, the execution is brilliant. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I had an idea somewhat similar to this before, but you've executed it in a way better than what I would have done.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really fucking cool. Also, write more stuff goddammit!

item.title: Excellent.
item.content:encoded:
Like I said in the chat, this uses site gimmicks to elevate a fairly mediocre idea into something that is awesome to read and interact with. Brilliant.

+1 with gusto!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a cute concept, but I think it would have been better served by a bit more buildup. The question of what's going on is answered almost as soon as it's raised, and all it evokes is a momentary flash of amusement. That's not enough to carry a story in my eyes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Note to self, do not betray the Chaos Insurgency.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah I have a real soft spot for scips that play around with the wiki format in a way that serves the concept. Done poorly it can be cringe-worthy or annoying, but the reason you take that risk is that the reward is correspondingly satisfying. +1 for sure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is the best use of a formatting gimmick I've seen in a long time. The fact that it has such awesome in-universe explanation is what really seals it. I liked this article prior to those formatting changes, but with them, it goes from good to great.

+1, with gusto

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Second SCP, here we go! I'd like to give a huge thanks to FortuneFavorsBold, Francis Scalia, ghostjulie, glewmie and MalyceGraves for helping me out with their feedback.

+ Show spoilers/explanation
- Hide spoilers/explanation

Hovering over some of the text/whitespace (or tapping it on mobile) will change it - there are two versions of the document. This might not look fantastic on mobile or when zoomed in on PC, sorry for that.

Image source is here, license is CC-BY-SA 2.0
